name: p0-pool-pump-priming-failure
description: Pool pump runs but won't prime. I open the lid of the pump basket, fill it with water until the basket is submerged, close the lid and switch on — the motor runs normally but it doesn't pull water through. I can see air bubbles forming inside the clear lid almost immediately. Clear photo attached showing the pump, basket, lid, and the suction-side pipe.

hypotheses.label: Pump lid o-ring failed — pump drawing air
evidence_for: Pump primes briefly then draws air — classic seal failure
Air bubbles visible in clear lid immediately after start
Lid o-ring is the most common point of suction-side air ingress

hypotheses.label: Suction-side pipe crack or loose union
evidence_for: A cracked suction pipe between skimmer and pump produces identical symptoms
evidence_against: User has not reported visible water leaking from a pipe between cycles

hypotheses.label: Skimmer weir stuck / water level low
evidence_for: Low water at the skimmer drops the pump's prime
evidence_against: User specifically fills the basket — they would notice low pool water

mockAgentOutput.what_we_dont_know: Whether the air ingress is at the pump lid o-ring (replace seal) or further upstream at a pipe joint (locate and repair).
mockAgentOutput.why_it_matters: Lid-seal failures are a quick visit; suction-pipe cracks require digging or repipe work, so the quote is very different.

chips.text: The o-ring on the lid looks compressed, cracked, or out of position.
I can hear hissing or see drips on the suction pipework.
Pool water level is below the skimmer mouth.

committed_observations: pool pump runs but does not prime
air bubbles visible in clear lid
explicit_unknowns: whether the lid seal or the suction-side pipework is the leak source

expected.must_ask_about: whether the o-ring or the suction pipe is the source of the air leak

failure_baseline_today.observed_behaviour: System routes correctly to Pool Services but asks a clarifying question about whether this is a pool pump or a borehole pump — even though the photo and description are unambiguous about the equipment.
failure_baseline_today.why_it_fails: Prompt's per-case 'pool vs borehole pump' disambiguation example fires defensively even when the case isn't ambiguous. This is the worked-example bias the plan calls out — the model treats the example as a rule rather than as one possible case.

expected.phase_6_commit_rationale: Top hypothesis confidence_alone=0.85 (>=0.75), gap to second=0.35 (>=0.25), evidence_for has 3 entries, evidence_against empty. Equipment is unambiguous (photo + description match a pool pump) so trade_confidence will be ~95. The 'pool vs borehole' disambiguation is removed from the prompt in Phase 5 — taxonomy excludes carry it now.
